message:
test(storage): assert list_issues priority/type filters return the right rows, not just "no error"

Follow-up of the assertion-hardening pass (bead p7xo). Two tests in
`tests/storage_list_filters.rs` called `storage.list_issues(&filters)`
for every enum variant and then discarded the `Result` with `let _`
— same smoke-test pattern fixed earlier in the progress-bar and
theme tests.

`filter_by_all_priority_levels`:
- Insert two issues (CRITICAL and BACKLOG) and assert that filtering
  by each `Priority` variant returns exactly the matching rows:
    * `priority == prio` for every returned issue.
    * Specifically: CRITICAL matches `[p0.id]`, BACKLOG matches
      `[p4.id]`, HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW return empty result sets.
  The prior "just verify no error" comment is obsolete and removed.

`filter_by_all_issue_types`:
- Similar treatment for the issue-type dimension: every filter
  result must be entirely the requested type. (The test fixture
  only seeds a Task issue, so Bug/Feature/Epic/etc. filters are
  asserted to return empty sets.)

Both tests now pin two invariants that were previously only
implicitly assumed: (1) the filter actually filters (doesn't
silently degenerate into "return everything"), and (2) no other
priority/type bleeds through. Future schema or query-planner
changes that regress either property will now fail these tests
instead of silently passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
